records.ety: [F. dboucher; pref. d- (L. dis- or de) + boucher to stop up, fr. bouche mouth, fr. L. bucca the cheek. Cf. Disembogue.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Debouched (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Debouching.]

defs: To issue; -- said of a stream passing from a gorge out into an open valley or a plain.
